description: Fresh fruits and vegetables for cooking, salads, and healthy snacks.
donor_guidance: Fresh or shelf-stable fruit and vegetables are both welcome.

description: Meat, poultry, fish, eggs, and plant-based proteins to help fill out meals.
donor_friendly_translation: Meat, fish, eggs, and plant protein
donor_guidance: Frozen, canned, or fresh protein all help — check expiration dates.

description: Milk, cheese, yogurt, and other refrigerated dairy for everyday meals.
donor_guidance: Refrigerated items only — please keep cold until drop-off.

description: Rice, pasta, bread, cereal, and other pantry staples that stretch groceries.
donor_guidance: Unopened packages preferred; whole-grain options are great.

description: Pantry basics without wheat or gluten — for celiac disease or gluten sensitivity.
donor_guidance: Look for a certified gluten-free label when possible.

description: Canned beans, soups, tomatoes, and other shelf-stable items that keep well.
donor_guidance: Pop-top or easy-open cans are especially helpful.

description: Formula, baby food, and infant supplies for families with young children.
donor_guidance: Unopened formula and age-appropriate baby food only.

description: Diapers and related infant supplies — a frequent high-need item for families.
donor_guidance: Any size is OK — unopened packages preferred.

description: Soap, toothpaste, toilet paper, and laundry supplies — not food, but essential for households.
donor_guidance: Travel-size and full-size items both help families.

description: Crackers, granola bars, juice, and other items for kids and quick bites between meals.
donor_guidance: Nut-free options are appreciated when available.
